question_text: Explain why a gas can be easily compressed, but a liquid cannot.
correct_answer: Gas particles are far apart with large spaces between them, so they can be pushed closer together. Liquid particles are already tightly packed with no empty spaces, so they cannot be compressed further.
explanation: Compressibility depends on how much empty space exists between particles. Gases have lots of space between particles, but liquids are already closely packed, so compression is impossible.
